mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 200k signals robust labor market momentum and consumer income expansion, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2015 payroll growth 211k indicates sustained hiring momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll months fail to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate remains near zero while inflation (CPI) stays below 2% year-over-year, signaling continued monetary accommodation. This low-rate regime compresses equity risk premiums and supports valuation multiples, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: zero-bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or the correlation between the accommodation signal and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows earnings disappointments, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity prices adjust downward.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2015 RSAFS at 441363M shows recent retail activity level
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with slowing consumer momentum
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS months fail to produce negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while remaining positive, signaling a slowdown in manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum. This foreshadows margin compression and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as growth expectations reset.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2015 INDPRO at 105.2161 reflects recent manufacturing activity
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with moderating supply growth
failure_condition: Deceleration periods produce positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC remains above -0.02 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1000k annualized units while real GDP growth remains above 2% annualized, signaling robust residential investment and forward-looking demand strength. This dual expansion drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through multiplier effects on construction, materials, and consumer wealth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 950k annualized AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2.0% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:February 2015 HOUST at 897k, Q4 2014 GDPC1 growth indicates expansion
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with accelerating housing and GDP growth
failure_condition: Dual-expansion periods produce negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and forward-looking recession risk. This triggers equity risk-off behavior and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:March 2015 UNRATE at 5.5% reflects recent labor market tightness
failure_condition: Unemployment spikes produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC > -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held constant near zero while core inflation (proxied by CPI) accelerates above 1.8% year-over-year, real rates become increasingly negative. This signals monetary policy is becoming accommodative relative to inflation, compressing real discount rates and supporting equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:April 2015 FEDFUNDS 0.11% with CPIAUCSL 235.186 implies negative real rates
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Negative real rate periods produce negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction signal shows no incremental explanatory power beyond FEDFUNDS alone (partial R² < 0.005).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth decelerates to below 2.0% annualized in the most recent quarter while retail sales growth remains positive, signaling a divergence where consumer spending is outpacing underlying economic capacity. This unsustainable divergence foreshadows demand destruction and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations reset downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 2.0% annualized AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:Q4 2014 GDPC1 at 16294.7B, March 2015 RSAFS at 441363M
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with diverging growth signals
failure_condition: Divergence periods produce positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (R² improvement < 0.01).
